content.thinking: Good, I can see the workspace diff. Now let me create a comprehensive PR based on all the changes. Let me look at the full diff to understand what was actually changed.

Now I can see all the changes. Let me summarize what was done:

1. Removed directory caching and project resolution logic from CLI hooks (dir-cache, getDirCacheEntry, setDirCacheEntry, etc.)
2. Removed `set-project` command and functionality
3. Removed `end-session` hook and session end functionality 
4. Removed `check-commit` pre-commit hook functionality
5. Removed git hook installation logic
6. Simplified `load-context` to just list workspace projects
7. Simplified `check-updates` to use workspace-level changes instead of project-specific
8. Updated CLAUDE.md with the new data model documentation
9. Updated timestamp in des-config.json

This is a significant refactor that removes a lot of the directory/project caching logic and session end reporting. Let me create a comprehensive PR description.

content.text: Now I'll create the PR with a comprehensive description covering all the changes:

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "Remove dir cache and project resolution from CLI hooks" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Removes directory-based project caching and session tracking from the CLI hooks, simplifying the Brain integration model. The system now relies on agents to infer project context from workspace metadata and task references instead of maintaining per-directory state.

## Changes

**CLI Commands (brain.ts, system.ts, git-hooks.ts):**
- Removed `brain system set-project <id>` command and directory cache logic
- Removed `brain system end-session` hook and session summary reporting
- Removed `brain check-commit` pre-commit hook analysis (now kept as deprecated no-op)
- Simplified `load-context` to list workspace projects instead of loading cached context
- Simplified `check-updates` to workspace-level change alerts (not project-specific)

**Git Hooks (cli/commands/init.ts):**
- Removed pre/post-commit hook installation logic from `brain init`
- Removed `SessionEnd` hook configuration from CLAUDE.md
- Removed legacy post-commit cleanup

**Configuration (cli/config.ts):**
- Removed `DirCacheEntry` type and all directory cache functions
- Removed `loadDirCache`, `saveDirCache`, `getDirCacheEntry`, `setDirCacheEntry`

**Documentation (cli/commands/init-content.ts):**
- Updated CLAUDE.md with comprehensive data model reference (entities, relationships, ID formats)
- Clarified project context inference strategies (task-scoped, directory-scoped, single-project, ambiguous)
- Updated hook descriptions to match new functionality
- Removed references to pre-commit hook analysis

## Rationale

Agents now infer project context dynamically from:
- Task ID lookups via `get_entity_detail` → traverses graph relationships
- Directory-to-project mapping conventions (services/packages in monorepos)
- Single-project workspaces (auto-selected)
- Explicit user clarification when ambiguous

This eliminates stale directory cache entries and simplifies the initialization flow.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
EOF
)"

content.thinking: Great! The PR was created successfully. Let me provide a summary to the user.

content.text:
PR created: **[#95 Remove dir cache and project resolution from CLI hooks](https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/pull/95)**

The PR consolidates a significant refactor that removes directory caching and session tracking from the CLI. Agents now infer project context dynamically from task IDs and workspace structure instead of maintaining per-directory state.
